mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in 65% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and sustains positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, forcing equity valuations lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, which immediately suppresses equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, lifting equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of periods following a housing starts drop greater than 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above its 12-month moving average during a post-shock recovery phase, indicating restored manufacturing capacity and immediate positive equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above its 12-month average during recovery phases is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases.
